boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Hello, World application demonstrating sending and receiving messages

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer can publish a message to a channel and receive it in real-time via subscription.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Console logs or UI updates showing the sent message and the received message event.
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully send and receive a real-time message using the PubNub JavaScript SDK.
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Hello, World example is the first documented complete round-trip of sending and receiving messages, marking the minimal viable success for using PubNub's real-time messaging.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has a modern web browser or Node.js environment
Developer has or will create a PubNub account
entry_point.developer_intent: Start building a real-time messaging application using PubNub's JavaScript SDK.

friction_gates.description: Developer must create a PubNub account to access keys and dashboard.
Creating an app in the Admin Portal is required to obtain publish and subscribe keys.
Installing the JavaScript SDK via CDN or npm is required to use PubNub in code.
Initializing the PubNub client with valid keys and user ID is required to connect to the service.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a PubNub account and sign in to the Admin Portal.
Create an app in the Admin Portal to obtain publish and subscribe keys.
Have a JavaScript runtime environment: modern browser, Node.js (v18+), or React Native setup.

primary_path.action: Create a PubNub account or sign in to the Admin Portal.
primary_path.input: User registration details or login credentials
primary_path.output: Access to PubNub Admin Portal dashboard
primary_path.success_signal: Dashboard loads successfully with app management options

primary_path.action: Create a new app in the Admin Portal to generate publish and subscribe keys.
details: Obtain publish and subscribe keys from the app dashboard
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Errors creating app or keys not generated

primary_path.action: Install the PubNub JavaScript SDK in the development environment.
details: For web: add SDK script tag from CDN
For Node.js or React Native: run npm install pubnub
primary_path.input: Development environment (web page or Node.js project)
primary_path.output: PubNub SDK available for use in code

primary_path.action: Initialize the PubNub client instance with publish and subscribe keys and a unique user ID.
details: Create PubNub instance with keys and userId
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Errors due to invalid keys or syntax

primary_path.action: Add event listeners to handle incoming messages and presence events.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No events received if listeners not properly registered
primary_path.success_signal: Listeners receive events when messages arrive

primary_path.action: Subscribe to a channel to receive messages.
details: Call subscribe method with channel name
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No messages received if subscription fails
primary_path.success_signal: Listener receives messages published to the channel

primary_path.action: Publish a message to the subscribed channel.
details: Call publish method with channel name and message payload
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Publish errors or no message received
primary_path.success_signal: Message received by listener and logged/displayed

sections_used: Tutorials and interactive live coding tour
sources.title: PubNub Developers: Build Powerful Real-Time Apps Easily | PubNub
Easily Explore, Learn, and Build our Documentation
JavaScript API & SDK Docs 10.2.9 | PubNub Docs

alternatives_considered.name: Core SDKs
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Core SDKs cover multiple languages and platforms but the JavaScript SDK has the most detailed step-by-step onboarding and is recommended for quick prototyping.

alternatives_considered.name: Chat SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Chat SDK is specialized and built on top of the JavaScript SDK; onboarding starts with the JavaScript SDK itself.

surface.name: JavaScript SDK
surface.selection_basis: The JavaScript SDK is prominently documented with detailed getting started guides and examples for web, Node.js, and React Native platforms, making it the primary surface for first-mile onboarding.
